During the course of the hearings on the new Ordinance, ir.

rental units in the City (four and ten respectively) . Each of
their properties is nonconforming as a result of changes in
developnent standards in 1955. Under the current zoning
ordinance, neither person would be able to rebuild the same

However, regulation section IB702.1(a) (3) now provides that.

what they will do is modify or exempt the application of the

sections.question: Does the development standards exception of regulation 1-8702.1 apply to the planning Commissioner and City council member who own in excess of three nonconforming rental units in the city?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether officials who own nonconforming residential rental properties in Maywood are subject to conflict-of-interest disqualification requirements under FPPC regulation 1-8702.1(a)(3)(E) when voting on zoning ordinance amendments that would benefit their properties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC from a city attorney, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document discusses proposed changes to zoning ordinance and conflict-of-interest implications under regulation 1-8702.1.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does the development standards exception of regulation 1-8702.1 apply to the planning Commissioner and City council member who own in excess of three nonconforming rental units in the city?

embedding.summary: City Attorney for Maywood requests FPPC opinion on whether two officials owning nonconforming rental properties must be disqualified from voting on proposed zoning ordinance amendments that would benefit their properties by exempting nonconforming units from amortization requirements.
